item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you ever read the books and/or watch the TV series Wayward Pines? it has extremely similar vibes to this skip which isn't a bad thing

I wonder what the procedural cartoons are like
Woo! We did it! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They should use the -3s combined with KALEIDOSCOPE to create more foundation personnel

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ironic that with all the scps that could end the world, the apocalypse was caused by a completely mundane super volcanic eruption

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, jugging by the air pollution combined with scp-2000’s destruction event indigo was probably the eruption of Yellowstone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To go into a little more detail, the implication is that SCP-2000 was destroyed in an eruption of a super volcano under Yellowstone Park, where the facilities were located. The ash in the air along with the physical destruction caused by the eruption is what caused the societal collapse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the in-SCP lore is that SCP-2000 was destroyed and that this site cannot contact anyone else, so they likely do not have access to SCP-2000's files at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't read through all the comments so perhaps this has already been addressed, but it sort of feels like this is somewhat of a knockoff of SCP 2000 (at least I think that's the one). Granted, this one is under the control of a guy who's bent on creating the ensuing people to fit what he remembers. Something that doesn't make sense though; They keep having these issues while theyre testing the system and then saying that every great work has flaws. The thing is, they've already BUILT this machine. In SCP 2000. And presumably, that had already been optimized. So why couldn't they take the data from that and the things they learned? With how similar the 2 operate, it would make sense if 2000 had already had many of the hickups this one had, especially considering that it had already been activated some number of times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd always thought that 2000 was built in the worst possible location.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know. The first image hooked me in, and I calmly glided through the special containment procedures and description. However, the system failure log dragged on for too long and didn't really catch my interest. Personally, it lagged the whole article behind.

However, the twist at the end made me go from downvote to novote. It's just really not my type of article, but the twist at the end made me go "Damn".

The clinical tone is fine, the concept is also good, but I don't know. It just seems as if it could be done in a shorter way than it already is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for the kind words, I really appreciate them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really took until the very end to fully pay off for me! I'm not well-versed on most Thaumiel SCPs but this one felt very unique, in the way the whole project was so completely abused by one person. It's such a simple revelation when it comes, and was beautifully set up, but still an effective twist.

And then there's the tone throughout the whole piece. That very "Foundation" style of grim duty, "the world's screwed but we still have a job to do" mentality. The logs were great. The last lines were excellent. Thank you for this entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Second image shows up fine for me. Maybe try bypassing your cache?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm … it's showing up fine for me. Not really sure what's going on with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For some reason, the second picture doesn’t show up in the article. The file is there, and if you click the area where it should be, you get sent to the wdfiles page for the image. It just doesn’t show up inside the article proper.

Just wanted to bring it to your attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really engaging, it all came together nicely at the end. Nice work! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indigo isn't really something I have plans to expand on at the moment, but it's possible to work out what it is from the way it's described ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, +1. Is Indigo something that's going to be expanded on?

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Told it would be great! Remember what I told you before about the redactions though! ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find it beyond amusing and beyond horrifying that these people are quibbling over the ethics of how to treat the -2s when they've already reduced them to programmable drones.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to AbsentmindedNihilist, dimadude and TrustyOlValet2 for their feedback in chat!

PLEASE, FOR THE LOVE OF GOD, DON’T SUE TANHONY
